MULTIPLE POSITIONS AVAILABLE

Employer: AMAZON WEB SERVICES, INC.
Offered Position: Principal, PMT - External Svcs
Job Location: Seattle, Washington
Job Number: AMZ9160351

Position Responsibilities:

Drive product management, including feature roadmap on behalf of customers in partnership with a highly-talented engineering team. Work closely with finance, marketing, business development, technical field community, and product management peers to ensure technical offerings and services have the right positioning, messaging, and enable compelling use cases across big data platforms. Drive rhythm of business and ensure delivery to support business goals. Define high-quality roadmaps based on team strategy and vision. Develop detailed business narratives and user stories that can be used to create pricing strategies, product specifications, and new features. Work closely with sales teams, solution architects, business development, and engineering teams to solve complex product and pricing issues. Manage prioritization and trade-offs between customer experience, performance, and operational support load.

Position Requirements:

Master's degree or foreign equivalent degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field and five years of experience in the job offered or a related occupation. Must have one year of experience in the following skill(s): (1) owning or driving roadmap strategy and definition for cloud storage products; (2) with feature delivery and tradeoffs of a product; and (3) technical product management.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
Position Requirements:

Bachelor’s degree or foreign equivalent degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field and five years of experience in the job offered or a related occupation. Employer will accept eight years of experience in the job offered or a related occupation as equivalent to a Bachelor’s degree and five years of experience. Must have five years of experience in at least one (1) of the following skills: 1) experience in construction management of large projects involving large-scale mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) plants; 2) general contractor and vendor management experience associated with construction and project execution (including experience with request for proposals, bidding, change orders, quality control, and RFI and submittal tracking); 3) experience with design or construction of data centers, critical infrastructure, or large-scale MEP plants; or 4) experience with systems, controls, commissioning, power management, and technical operations of data centers critical infrastructure, or large-scale MEP plants.#0000
jobs.description:
MULTIPLE POSITIONS AVAILABLE

Employer: AMAZON DATA SERVICES, INC.
Offered Position: Tech Ops Engineer II
Job Location: Stafford, Virginia
Job Number: AMZ9477147

Position Responsibilities:

Responsible for construction project management and oversight of construction related activities as they relate to new builds or general capital projects which includes the ownership of the project scope, quality, schedule, and budget. Direct interface with construction general contractors during the bidding, award, execution, and project closeout/punch list phases. Conduct negotiations with general contractors and evaluate bids/proposals with detail and accuracy. Create construction project scope and request for proposals. Conduct financial analysis of construction. Responsible for construction project management for specific initiatives aimed at increasing the resiliency of data centers. Constructability reviews of electrical and mechanical designs associated with the construction of new data centers or optimization of existing data centers. Construction document management including submittal review, RFI’s, change orders, and invoicing. Construction project quality control. Record and report key construction metrics to team members and management. Drive teams to perform troubleshooting and root-cause failure analysis associated with equipment failures. Be a leader within the group as well as within internal and external teams that support the data center.

Position Requirements:

Bachelor’s degree or foreign equivalent degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field and five years of experience in the job offered or a related occupation. Employer will accept eight years of experience in the job offered or a related occupation as equivalent to a Bachelor’s degree and five years of experience. Must have five years of experience in at least one (1) of the following skills: 1) experience in construction management of large projects involving large-scale mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) plants; 2) general contractor and vendor management experience associated with construction and project execution (including experience with request for proposals, bidding, change orders, quality control, and RFI and submittal tracking); 3) experience with design or construction of data centers, critical infrastructure, or large-scale MEP plants; or 4) experience with systems, controls, commissioning, power management, and technical operations of data centers critical infrastructure, or large-scale MEP plants.

jobs.description:
MULTIPLE POSITIONS AVAILABLE

Employer: AMAZON.COM SERVICES LLC
Offered Position: Sr. Mgr, Tech Infra Prog Mgr
Job Location: Seattle, Washington
Job Number: AMZ9497375

Position Responsibilities:

Execute planning processes resulting in strategic alignment across multiple stakeholders to develop infrastructure capacity plans for the acquisition of data center space and power. Establish capacity plans through forecasting process and manage strategic execution, including supporting the acquisition of long lead infrastructure for new regions. Dive deep into planning models to identify risks and opportunities for region health and resolve constraints by negotiating across demand and supply owners. Conduct modeling and scenario analysis to make data-driven recommendations that inform capacity and capital expenditure decisions. Clearly define data requirements and metrics, working with teams to implement the entire data flow from capture to presentation and management. Partner with key stakeholders to bridge changes in demand, addressing short and long-term capacity constraints, and writing supporting documentation for capital expenditures. Identifying opportunities to invent and simplify processes, identifying business risks and implementing resolutions and scalable mechanisms. Drive activities required to create capital expenditure plans for data center infrastructure globally. Drive accountability across the Infrastructure organization and develop mechanisms to monitor and escalate against the health of the infrastructure business to executive leadership. Manage a team of seven employees and develop a high performing team.

Position Requirements:

Bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent degree in Engineering, Business, Supply Chain, or a related field and nine years of progressive post-baccalaureate experience in the job offered or a related occupation. Must have five years of experience in the following skills: leading and developing teams. Must also have three years of experience in the following skills: experience with demand, supply, and capacity planning; owning program strategy, end-to-end delivery, and communicating results to executive leadership; and using data and metrics to drive improvements. Up to 25% domestic and international travel to perform role responsibilities may be required.

jobs.description:
At Amazon, our mission is to be Earth’s most customer-centric company. To achieve this goal, we strive to exceed expectations by innovating and providing best-in-class customer support as we expand our logistics products and services including improving how we fulfill and deliver customer orders. We’re making history and the good news is that we’ve only just begun. At Amazon you get to Work with smart, passionate people who are building new products and services every day on behalf of our customers. As a Delivery Station Customer Service Associate, you will help improve the customer experience by providing in station real-time, hands-on support to ensure our customers receive their packages on time, as promised. The role will also help connect customers with experts who can provide them the most accurate and up-to-date information about their packages. The work is beyond fulfilling and delivering customer orders; it’s making sure we put smiles on customer’s faces.

Key job responsibilities
As a Delivery Station Customer Service Associate, you will be responsible for:
• Communicating with customers directly in-person, in addition to communicating via phone and email
• Empathizing with and prioritizing customer needs
• Upholding company values and respecting every customer
• Resolving issues and setting appropriate expectations with customers
• Clearly understanding and responding appropriately to the issues that customers present
• Consistently composing grammatically correct, concise, and accurate written responses to customer issues
• Approaching problems logically and with good judgment to ensure the appropriate customer outcome
• Making quick and effective decisions on behalf of the customer
• Working a flexible Full-Time (40+ hours per week) schedule
Performing the following tasks, with or without reasonable accommodation
• Carry, lift, push and pull up to 49 pounds
• Frequently push, pull, squat, bend and reach
• Stand and walk during shifts lasting up to 12 hours
• Work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud (hearing protection will be provided)
• Work in an environment where the temperature may vary due to outside weather conditions and is not controlled

A day in the life
You’ll be based at one of our last-mile delivery warehouses, where Amazon’s leading logistics system operates. As orders are dispatched and delivered, you’ll be in contact with our warehouse, associates, delivery partners and customers when they need guidance. Where a customer needs to reschedule a delivery, you’ll be informing our delivery partners. Where you notice a delivery issue, you’ll get to work resolving it straight away, so that our customers get a great experience every time without having to contact Amazon. Operating at the heart of Amazon’s logistics, you’ll develop logistical and customer expertise that sets Amazon apart. You’ll connect with our customers every day, using your in-depth understanding of our supply chain and local knowledge of delivery connections and warehouses.

About the team
Our mission is to be Earth’s most customer-centric company, and few departments have as direct an impact on that mission as our Customer Service team. We're an inclusive team who empower and look after our people, so they have the time and energy to focus on our customers’ happiness. Our goal is to reduce customer effort every way we can, helping them get the best results quickly and efficiently. We support our self-service channels to offer expert insights into our customers’ most complex issues. Within Customer Service, there are opportunities in process improvement, customer experience, data analysis and customer retention. The CS team is always looking for ways to make Amazon’s customer service even better, and we need excellent people to make that happen.

jobs.description:
Here at Amazon, we embrace our differences. We are committed to furthering our culture of diversity and inclusion of our teams within the organization.

How do you get items to customers quickly, cost-effectively, and—most importantly—safely, in less than an hour? And how do you do it in a way that can scale? Our teams of hundreds of scientists, engineers, aerospace professionals, and futurists have been working hard to do just that! We are delivering to customers and are excited for what’s to come. Check out more information about Prime Air on the About Amazon blog (https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/transportation/amazon-prime-air-delivery-drone-reveal-photos).

We’re working on the future. If you are seeking an iterative fast-paced environment where you can drive innovation, apply state-of-the-art technologies to solve real world challenges, and provide visible benefit to customers this is your opportunity.

Come work on the Amazon Prime Air team!

We're looking for a Senior Configuration Control Specialist who combines superb technical, research and analytical capabilities with a demonstrated ability to get the right things done quickly and effectively. This person must be comfortable working with a team of top-notch engineers and collaborating with our research, manufacturing and operations teams. We’re looking for people who innovate and love solving hard problems! You will work hard, have fun, and of course, benefit our customers everywhere!

This position requires a candidate that can operate in an ambiguous environment. The ideal candidate will have a strong technical background in multiple engineering disciplines. This role will interface closely with Program, Engineering (HW/SW), Manufacturing, Quality, Product Support and Operations across a broad range of technical areas to enable robust configuration management mechanisms across Prime Air's ecosystem. This person will be required to articulate when gaps are identified and propose solutions quickly.

The ideal candidate will have a balance of breadth across engineering disciplines, depth and expertise in select areas, and practical real-life experience with configuration management of complex systems.

A day in the life
As a Prime Air Senior Configuration Management specialist, you’ll find yourself at the intersection of engineering, systems, and execution. On any given day, you might:

- Meet with Engineering, Manufacturing, Product and/or Operations to review upcoming hardware changes and understand their impact across the enterprise

- Align with downstream teams to evaluate when a change should be implemented based on material availability, testing status, integration readiness and regulatory concurrence.

- Work with the Infrastructure tools team to refine toolset process to improves visibility into configuration state across the Prime Air program

- Dig into the details of proposed change request to ensure documentation is complete and the change impact is properly captured before being brought to Prime Air's Configuration Control Board (CCB).

- Facilitate change request working sessions to improve process workflows to help streamline integration efforts.

- Collaborate with TPMs and program leads to ensure configuration integrity as systems evolve over time

- Translate technical requirements into roadmap input for PLM or PDM systems

- Provide input to executive reviews on how configuration decisions affect vehicle effectivity and delivery timelines

About the team
The Configuration Management, Facilities & Infrastructure Tools (CMFIT) team defines, deploys and refines Prime Air’s process controls and tool sets required for the definition, manufacture, qualification, operations and configuration management of Prime Air’s fast growing ultra-fast delivery platform with target of delivering to 500 million packages a year to customer around the world before the end of the decade.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 7+ years of increasingly responsible roles in safety and/or environmental programs within manufacturing, production, service operations, or other relevant experience
- Experience in safety and/or environmental programs in manufacturing, production, service operations, or related fields
- Bachelor's degree or above
- 5+ years of management experience
jobs.description:
Here at Amazon, we embrace our differences. We are committed to furthering our culture of diversity and inclusion of our teams within the organization.

How do you get items to customers quickly, cost-effectively, and—most importantly—safely, in less than an hour? And how do you do it in a way that can scale? Our teams of hundreds of scientists, engineers, aerospace professionals, and futurists have been working hard to do just that! We are delivering to customers, and are excited for what’s to come. Check out more information about Prime Air on the About Amazon blog (https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/transportation/amazon-prime-air-delivery-drone-reveal-photos).

If you are seeking an iterative environment where you can drive innovation, apply state-of-the-art technologies to solve real world delivery challenges, and provide benefits to customers, Prime Air is the place for you.

Come work on the Amazon Prime Air Team!

We are looking for a Senior Safety Manager to join our Prime Air team and ensure the safety and compliance of our operations. As the Safety Leader for Prime Air, you will partner with various stakeholders such as site operations teams, Engineering, Legal, Human Resources, Ergonomics, Workplace Health & Safety (WHS), Environmental Program Managers and others, to execute safety policies and adhere to all applicable local, regional, and Amazon regulations. You will lead continuous improvement initiatives to reduce safety and ergonomic risk in our processes and create a safe and healthful working environment for our Associates.
As a Senior Safety Manager, you will have responsibility for management and IC personnel deployed across the US and internationally. You will communicate effectively and clearly with cross functional Teams and influence change through providing comprehensive risk assessments and safety data analysis. You will instruct and train Operations Leaders in safety policies and assist the operations site teams in incorporating our safety standards at their site. You will identify best practices and incorporate them into our standards to continuously improve company safety policies. You will train and drive compliance at multiple locations.
You will create and execute leadership development plans for the Safety Associates on your team, communicating safety team expectations and giving frequent and appropriate feedback. You will ensure they are executing the core competencies and leadership principles of an Amazon safety professional.
You will support multiple projects at once in a complex and ambiguous operating environment. You will have a deep understanding of the particularities of the regions your teams support and be able to make decisions with appropriate justification based on data, facts, or legislation. You will negotiate resources and priorities, escalate effectively, and find a path forward in difficult situations. You will deliver results independently and with limited guidance.

Export Control License: This position may require a deemed export control license for compliance with applicable laws and regulations. Placement is contingent on Amazon’s ability to apply for and obtain an export control license on your behalf.


Key job responsibilities
• Oversee execution of Prime Air's safety program, ensuring compliance with all applicable Amazon WHS & EHS Policies and local/regional Health and Safety standards across all Prime Air sites. Train and educate site leadership and Associates on their responsibilities under each policy
• Lead continuous improvement initiatives to reduce conditional and ergonomic risk in our processes and ensure a safe and healthful working environment for our associates
• Develop and implement safety policies and procedures that comply with all applicable Amazon WHS Policies and local/regional regulations
• Monitor and evaluate the safety performance of the Prime Air operations and identify areas for improvement and risk reduction
• Communicate effectively and collaboratively with various stakeholders in Prime Air, such as site operations teams, Engineering, Legal, Human Resources, and others, to provide comprehensive risk assessments and safety data analysis, influence change and promote a safety culture, execute the Safety Management System, report and communicate safety performance metrics, and give feedback and suggestions
• Train and coach Operations Leaders and associates on safety policies and best practices and assist the operations site teams in incorporating our safety standards at their site
• Lead a team of safety team members that consists of managers, WHS/EHS specialists, and Safety and Compliance Officers (SCOs). Also work closely with the Prime Air Aviation safety personnel
• Create and execute leadership development plans for your Safety Associates on your team, communicate safety team expectations, and give frequent and appropriate feedback to them
• Support multiple projects at once and handle complexities and ambiguity with minimal guidance
• Make data-driven and evidence-based decisions and justify them with appropriate rationale and documentation
• Negotiate resources and priorities with stakeholders, escalate issues to senior management in a timely manner, and propose solutions within the project scope and timeline
• Conduct regular and comprehensive site WHS and safety audits to ensure accuracy and compliance of recordkeeping, workplace organization, associate behaviors, equipment, and processes to global WHS and safety standards and local regulations and implement solutions to eliminate exposure to risks and prevent injury at all sites
• Act as the representative for government regulators and address any issues that arise on a site level for that region
• Assist in the investigation of the root cause Health and Safety issues put forward by Prime Air employees
• Support new launch sites including initial site inspections, identifying accountability, purchasing and site readiness review

jobs.description:
Operations is at the heart of Amazon’s business. We are known for our speed, accuracy, and exceptional service. Our buildings deliver tens of thousands of products to hundreds of countries worldwide, every day. The Reliability & Maintenance Engineering (RME) team are the business partners that work tirelessly behind the scenes to make it happen. We drive continuous improvement, and maintain all of the robotics and material handling equipment (MHE) to ensure our customers are met with the Amazon smile. Come join us on our journey!

About the Role:
As an Automation Engineer, you will play a crucial role in maximizing equipment reliability and operational performance in our fulfillment centers by providing proactive controls automation and technical support. Your expertise will be essential in troubleshooting complex control systems, analyzing performance metrics, and driving continuous improvement initiatives. You'll collaborate with cross-functional teams to optimize material handling equipment (MHE) systems and implement innovative projects. You'll work with state-of-the-art technology, including conveyors, sortation systems, scanners, cameras, print and apply systems, and SCADA devices and programs. Through your efforts, you'll directly contribute to ensuring our customers receive their orders with Amazon's signature speed and accuracy.

What Do We Offer?
Amazon offers competitive compensation packages including comprehensive healthcare benefits starting on Day 1, matching 401(k) program, and up to 20 weeks of paid parental leave. But wait, there’s more: We provide lightweight fire-resistant daily wear and composite toe safety shoes for your safety!


Key job responsibilities
Key job responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
- You will serve as the site expert for maintaining the automation systems that are critical for operations
- You will understand, maintain and troubleshoot material handling control systems, including PLC/PC controllers and industrial networks such as Ethernet, ControlNet, DeviceNet, Profibus, motor control systems, servo drives, frequency drives, and electrical distribution systems
- You will monitor MHE metrics and partner with maintenance/operations to address system performance issues and provide analysis across all operations
- You will provide first-level escalation support for site technicians, both locally and remotely
- You will perform and analyze building System Assessments to enhance material handling system performance

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelors or Master’s degree in EE, CE, or CS
- Minimum of 3+ years in developing design methodology or CAD flows in synthesis, PNR, or sign-off areas for advanced technology nodes.
- Experience in writing production scripts for implementation and sign-of. tools in TCL, Perl, and/or Python
- Solid understanding of ASIC physical design, physical design flows, and methodologies including synthesis, place and route, STA, formal verification.
- Proven track record of delivering metric driven PPA flow development and support.
jobs.description:
Amazon Web Services provides a highly reliable, scalable, low-cost infrastructure platform in the cloud that powers hundreds of thousands of businesses in 190 countries around the world. AWS has the broadest and deepest set of machine learning and AI services for our customers’ businesses. We are seeking experienced Physical Design Engineer to build the next generation of our cloud server platforms. Our success depends on our world-class infrastructure; we’re handling massive scale and rapid integration of emergent technologies.

As a member of the Cloud-Scale Machine Learning Acceleration team you’ll be responsible for the design and optimization of Hardware in our data centers including technologies such as AWS Inferentia which is a machine learning inference product designed to deliver high performance at low cost.

You’ll provide leadership in the application of new technologies to large scale deployments in a continuous effort to deliver a world-class customer experience. This is a fast-paced, intellectually challenging position, and you’ll work with thought-leaders in multiple technology areas. You’ll have relentlessly high standards for yourself and everyone you work with, and you’ll be constantly looking for ways to improve our products' performance, quality and cost. We’re changing an industry, and we want individuals who are ready for this challenge and want to reach beyond what is possible today.

Key job responsibilities
- You will create and support innovative physical design methodology and CAD flows.
- Develop cloud infrastructure to support physical design work.
- Drive improvement in RTL2GDS flows/methodology for PPA and TAT improvement.
- Create Dashboard/central reports for project tracking and visualizing QoR/stats
- Interface directly with RTL, Physical Design, Package Design, DFT and other teams to improve methodologies and efficiencies and drive efforts to resolution.
- Work with EDA tool vendors to evaluate new tools, solve bugs, improve usability, etc.
